title: lathyrus × tournefortii
intro: Lathyrus × tournefortii, also known as the yellow sweet pea or the French pease, is a hybrid plant species of the pea family, resulting from the cross between Lathyrus odoratus and Lathyrus clymenum. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it contains isoflavones and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Consuming the plant's seeds can provide health benefits such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing menopausal symptoms, and supporting bone health. The seeds can be prepared as an herbal remedy, often dried and used in teas, infusions, or capsules, allowing individuals to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
